summary: Large gm_18A5 tournament-mode decomp slice. The PR replaced many `/// #` stubs or partials in `src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c`, added typed bracket/static-data layouts, corrected `TmData` tournament menu records, and updated headers/prototypes. decomp.dev reported 57.13% matched code overall, +0.20% and +7748 bytes, with 18 new matches and 46 unmatched-item improvements. The human PR body only says `Tracking PR as usual`, so technical intent is inferred from the diff and bot report rather than review discussion.

key_files.role: Main decomp body for tournament mode
key_files.evidence: 5797 additions/1125 deletions across 57 hunks; added C for bracket setup, drawing, UI callbacks, menu input, results, preload/audio, and state handlers.
Added `BracketSrcEntry`, `BracketEntry`, `lbl_80473AB8[0x40]`, `lbl_803D9E9C[0x70]`, and `lbl_803DA0D0_t` with a size assert.
Replaced many `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with concrete signatures such as `fn_8018A514(int, float)`, `fn_8018AA74(HSD_JObj*, s32, s32)`, and `fn_8018E85C(DynamicModelDesc*, s32)`.
Changed `TmData` around `x37` to a packed `TmUnkMenuData` array of 64 entries with fields through `xF`, and adjusted padding to `x4B8`.
`fn_8019CDBC` now uses the corrected `TmData.x37` fields (`xE`, `xF`, `x5`, `x3`, `x7`) and `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj`.

what_changed.details: Added tournament bracket functions including `fn_8018A514`, `fn_8018AA74`, `fn_8018C8D4`, `fn_8018D50C`, `fn_8018DC18`, `fn_8018DF68`, `fn_8018E618`, and `fn_8018E85C`; comments identify bracket line drawing, connector drawing, camera setup, and JObj placement.

Implemented or improved menu/JObj callbacks and input handlers such as `fn_801913BC`, `fn_80191D38`, `fn_80191E9C`, `fn_8019237C`, `fn_80194D84`, `fn_80194F30`, `fn_801953C8`, `fn_80195CCC`, and `fn_8019610C`.

`fn_8019B458` now transitions to the results screen, ranks players using `lbl_803DA0D0.rank_thresholds`, preloads stage/character cache data through `lbDvd`, and prepares audio masks with `lbAudioAx`.

The PR converted raw byte/anonymous layouts into named structs and updated external prototypes so later code can use typed `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_JObj*`, `DynamicModelDesc*`, `ResultsData*`, and `TmData*` instead of generic unknowns.

smart_moves: Promoted `lbl_80473AB8` from a small anonymous byte-offset struct to a full `BracketEntry` layout with explicit offsets and a `GXColor` field, unlocking cleaner bracket drawing and slot update code while preserving unknown offset names.
Modeled source bracket records separately as `BracketSrcEntry`, letting `fn_8018A514` copy ROM/static source data into live `BracketEntry` records field-by-field.
Corrected `TmData.x37` as a packed 64-entry `TmUnkMenuData` array of 0x12-byte records; the follow-through fix in `gmtou.c` shows the layout change was applied to cross-file users.
Used typed asset/model access where possible, for example changing `lbl_804D6670->models[4]` into the call to `fn_8018E85C` instead of raw pointer arithmetic.
Kept known partial-match status visible with `@todo` notes for register allocation or instruction scheduling rather than pretending all rewritten C was complete.
Introduced `lbl_803DA0D0_t` with a `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x184)`, which is a reusable pattern for decomping opaque rodata tables safely.
decomp_lessons: For UI-heavy game-mode files, investing in static data layouts can produce many downstream matches because rendering, input, and state callbacks all share the same offsets.
Packed struct layout matters: changing `TmData.x37` from an undersized/loosely padded block to packed 64 records required updating all field consumers, not just the file being decompiled.
Concrete prototypes in public headers reduce ambiguity and make cross-file recompilation expose layout/signature mistakes earlier.
When field purposes are still unknown, offset-style names like `x4C`, `x78`, `xA4`, and `xD0` are acceptable if the struct offsets are explicit and stable.
Documenting match blockers such as register allocation, loop pattern, and instruction scheduling helps future permuter/manual work resume from the right constraint.
Use rodata labels for matching-sensitive float constants when literals change load/scheduling behavior; the diff shows constants such as `lbl_804DA6A0` and `lbl_804DA810` being used.
naming_conventions: `gm_18A5` retains address-based function names (`fn_8018...`, `gm_8019...`) where semantic names are not yet known.
Game-mode lifecycle callbacks use descriptive suffixes when known, e.g. `gm_8019628C_OnFrame`, `gm_801963B4_OnEnter`, `gm_801964A4_OnLeave`, and `gm_8019B2DC_OnFrame`.
New local/static types use descriptive or label-derived names: `BracketEntry`, `BracketSrcEntry`, `CamDesc`, `Lbl804799B8_t`, `Lbl804799D8_t`, and `lbl_803DA0D0_t`.

Unknown struct fields keep offset names (`x2E`, `x37`, `x4B8`, `x4C`, `x78`, `xA4`) while partially known high-level fields like `entrants`, `cur_option`, `stage_selection_type`, and `cpu_level` are preserved.
Common local variable names follow project decomp style: `gobj`, `jobj`, `tm`/`tmd`, `entry`/`ent`, `state_ptr`, `buttons`, and `trigger`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Frequent `PAD_STACK` calls with exact byte counts were used to control stack frame shape.
Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma auto_inline off`, `#pragma dont_inline on/off`, and `#pragma inline_depth(0)` around sensitive functions.
Used `M2C_FIELD` for unresolved global/entity offsets in `fn_8018E618`, especially around `HSD_GObj_Entities` lists.
Comments explicitly note type casts used to match target instruction patterns such as `stw`, `sth`, and `stb`.
Several remaining `@todo` comments identify the likely cause of mismatch: register allocation, loop pattern, minor register allocation, or instruction scheduling.
Switched some raw/literal accesses to typed or rodata-label accesses, such as `lbl_804D6670->models[4]` and `lbl_804DA810`, to match generated code patterns.

review_feedback.feedback: Only `Tracking PR as usual`; no detailed rationale was provided.
GALE01 report showed +7748 matched bytes, 18 new matches, and 46 improvements in unmatched items.
No human reviews or inline review comments were present in the slice, so there is no captured reviewer guidance to reuse.
